title: Ceropegia ciliatioris
taxonomy: Ceropegia ciliatioris belongs to the Apocynaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its flower structure and leaf morphology. The genus Ceropegia is a group of flowering plants native to southern Africa and the Indian subcontinent, and Ceropegia ciliatioris is a species within this genus. The species Ceropegia ciliatioris is further classified as a succulent, indicating its adapted growth habit to conserve water in arid environments. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Ceropegia ciliatioris and other related species.
